text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Willow Grove, there was a squirrel named Holly. Holly was the youngest member of the woodland animals' club. Every Saturday afternoon she'd gather her friends around the ancient oak: wise old owl Owlchieko, graceful doe Lily, stealthy fox Toby, and the ever-loving cat Patchwayedlae.

They'd listen to Owlchieko's tales of ancient myths, dance to Lily's dreamy melodies, explore Toby's secret berry grove, and steal glances at Patchwayedlae's intricate pottery-making. Their favorite game was tag. Holly was the fastest squirrel, but Lily often surprised her.

One crisp autumn morning, a fox named Felix bounded into the club. Felix boasted an enchanting voice that could sway the leaves. He decided today was the day he'd convince Holly to chase him through the enchanted forest. As usual, Holly agreed.
